query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "mega-asr: towards in-the-wild^2 speech recognition via scaling up real" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (self-supervised speech pre-training methods like wav2vec 2.0 and XLSR) and measures the same dependent variable (Automatic Speech Recognition performance/WER) within the same domain (speech recognition), even though it focuses on a specific low-resource language (Flemish Dutch) rather than the "in-the-wild" scaling aspect of the user's specific title. It provides essential empirical evidence on the effectivenes
